habit_description: Ensure your “Do Now” task is already displayed or handed out before students arrive so they can begin immediately.
habit_text: Do Now on board or distributed.

habit_description: Quietly scan each pupil’s uniform as they enter and use discreet gestures or a brief prompt to correct any issues.
habit_text: Uniforms checked and corrected silently.

habit_description: Make sure you stand at the classroom door and personally welcome every student with eye contact and a smile.
habit_text: Teacher positioned at the threshold, greeting each pupil.

habit_description: Start a clear countdown (e.g., “20…19…18…”) as soon as everyone is in, to build urgency and focus.
habit_text: Countdown used.

habit_description: Check that all pupils are writing or answering by twenty seconds into the task, and give an immediate warning if anyone isn’t.
habit_text: Students working within 20 seconds.

habit_description: Confirm that your two pre-assigned book-handlers distribute exercise books quickly and accurately before the countdown begins.
habit_text: Exercise books handed out by designated students.

habit_description: Enforce a rapid transition into silence, with every pupil seated and focused no more than five seconds after the final student enters.
habit_text: All students seated silently within 5 seconds.

habit_description: Choose and stick to a single modelling approach (e.g., “Stop and Jot” or “Live Model”) rather than switching methods mid-lesson.
habit_text: One clear strategy of instruction is being used.

habit_description: Check that every student is engaged—writing their notes, repeating key phrases, or contributing to the shared model.
habit_text: Pupils are actively participating (jotting, repeating, constructing).

habit_description: Display each step of your thinking clearly on the board, using a consistent framework (e.g., “Step 1… Step 2…”).
habit_text: Modelling is visible and structured.

habit_description: Cold-call different students regularly to confirm they’ve grasped each point before moving on.
habit_text: Teacher checks for accuracy and understanding throughout.

habit_description: Embed a check (e.g., mini-whiteboards, turn-and-talk or exit tickets) at least once every five minutes.
habit_text: At least one strategy in use.

habit_description: After an initial answer, ask “Why?” or “Can you explain your reasoning?” to push for deeper analysis.
habit_text: Follow-up questions deepen thinking.

habit_description: As soon as you spot an error, pause the class briefly to correct the misunderstanding on the spot.
habit_text: Misconceptions are addressed instantly.

habit_description: Ensure you select reluctant or quieter students explicitly—no one should be able to sit out.
habit_text: Every pupil is participating (no opt out).

habit_description: Check that each student is working on the Core, Stretch or Twist activity you intended for their level.
habit_text: Pupils are completing core, stretch and twist tasks.

habit_description: Look for and praise evidence of student persistence—note who sticks with the toughest questions.
habit_text: Pupils are resilient and focused on task.

habit_description: During the independent challenge, circulate silently—do not prompt or clarify unless it’s part of your diagnostic plan.
habit_text: Silent Solo is observed with no help offered.

habit_description: Write the time allowed on the board and count down; stop the task exactly when time is up.
habit_text: Timings are visible and enforced.

habit_description: Signal clearly when there are two minutes left and insist everyone packs up without speaking.
habit_text: Pupils pack away in silence.

habit_description: Ensure your designated book-collectors gather and stack exercise books swiftly while others tidy.
habit_text: Books collected efficiently by named pupils.

habit_description: Dismiss row by row with eye contact and a positive phrase, ensuring they leave the room silently.
habit_text: Pupils dismissed in controlled and silent fashion.

habit_description: End with a brief summary or praise statement—keep it under 15 seconds and very focused.
habit_text: Teacher delivers calm, purposeful closing message.

habit_description: Stand at the door to monitor and praise correct corridor conduct as students leave.
habit_text: Corridor behaviour calm and compliant upon exit.

habit_description: Use a steady, authoritative tone to give each warning—avoid raising your voice or debating the consequence.
habit_text: Warnings delivered calmly and clearly.

habit_description: Aim to catch and praise at least four positive behaviours for every correction you issue.
habit_text: Praise-to-warning ratio of 4:1 observed.

habit_description: Remain composed and confident—even under challenge—to reinforce your status and defuse tension.
habit_text: Teacher maintains calm authority throughout.

habit_description: Stick to the agreed system: once a warning is given, do not engage in debate—apply the next sanction if needed.
habit_text: No negotiation of consequences or arguing.

habit_description: Use silent cues or brief verbal reminders to bring any off-task student swiftly back on track.
habit_text: Pupils consistently corrected and re-engaged.

habit_description: Set a visible timer for each activity and move the class on instantly when it rings.
habit_text: Lesson transitions are timed and prompt.

habit_description: Eliminate any dead time between tasks so every second is allocated to learning.
habit_text: No wasted time—pupils are always working.

habit_description: Stay standing, circulate purposefully around the room, and scan students’ work continuously.
habit_text: Teacher remains on feet and actively circulates.

habit_description: Open the lesson with a firm, controlled voice and purposeful posture to set the tone.
habit_text: Clear tone and presence established early.

habit_description: Check that pairs follow the agreed protocol—no interrupting, one speaker at a time.
habit_text: Talk routines observed (e.g., turn and talk).

habit_description: Prompt students to restate their answers in full, structured sentences rather than single words.
habit_text: Pupils using full sentences.

habit_description: Listen for imprecise terms and model the exact subject-specific word immediately.
habit_text: Vocabulary corrected or refined during discussion.

habit_description: Use advanced terminology and clear syntax yourself—never simplify the language for its own sake.
habit_text: Teacher models high-quality academic language.

habit_description: Cold-call random students to share their recall answers—no hands-up.
habit_text: All pupils participate.

habit_description: Scan responses quickly and correct errors on the spot rather than waiting until later.
habit_text: Corrections are immediate.

habit_description: Display the starter quiz or blank-page test clearly on the board with instructions before you begin.
habit_text: Retrieval task is visible and structured.

habit_description: Press students for accurate spellings or definitions—praise exact recall.
habit_text: Vocabulary and facts recalled precisely.

habit_description: Issue the “Give your Best Self” command and scan the room, pausing to affirm correct posture.
habit_text: All students are sat up straight with pens down and hands on the desk.

habit_description: If a student doesn’t comply within two seconds, give a brief reminder or warning.
habit_text: Sanctions are given swiftly for non-compliance.

habit_description: Circulate or scan front to back to verify everyone is fully engaged in the posture routine.
habit_text: Teacher checks for compliance quickly and efficiently.

habit_description: Verbally acknowledge individuals immediately when they adopt Best Self posture.
habit_text: Teacher praises the students who are doing the right thing.

habit_description: Acknowledge students who move the ruler line-by-line with accuracy and focus.
habit_text: Teacher praises those demonstrating precise tracking.

habit_description: Remind pupils to position their ruler correctly under the first line before “Begin Reading Routine.”
habit_text: All students have rulers in place and are tracking the text.

habit_description: If you spot a pupil not tracking, deliver a quick, firm reminder to resume correctly.
habit_text: Sanctions issued immediately for any non-compliance.

habit_description: Circulate promptly—stand behind each row to confirm rulers are tracking the text line by line.
habit_text: Teacher scans quickly for correct setup and movement between lines.
